speaker: Luis de Guindos
tone_summary: De Guindos delivered his one readable speech in the window — the Ragnar Nurkse Memorial Lecture in Tallinn — as a broad macro-stability assessment rather than a monetary policy signal. He acknowledged a positive baseline: euro area growth of 1.5% in 2025, falling inflation, resilient labour markets. But he flagged deep structural uncertainty from US trade policy shifts, the erosion of multilateral norms, and the increased defence and energy spending burden on European fiscal positions. He explicitly endorsed the ECB's 'data-dependent, meeting-by-meeting' framework as appropriate given uncertainty, without pointing toward a specific next move. His financial stability lens means he is attentive to second-round effects and tail risks, but his January speech (Monetary policy and financial stability in the euro area) similarly showed no strong rate direction. Score near neutral, stable.
representative_quote: A new paradigm has emerged, where the rule of law is challenged by the rule of power. High uncertainty and geoeconomic fragmentation complicate the assessment of the economic outlook.
